title: LyGo Cloning Protocol
author: Kristoffer Bach Falkenberg
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdmqi45w

steps.instruction: Prepare a reaction mix in the following proportions: %s %s ComponentVolume (uL)Vector1gBlock/PCR product3FastDigest SapI0.510x T4 ligase110x FD buffer110x T4 buffer1MQ2.5 The exact volume of LyGo vector and LyGo fragment depends on their kb sizes and DNA concentrations. The optimal molar ratio of vector:fragment is between 1:3 and 1:6, so dilutions/additions should be made accordingly. The NEB Ligation Calculator ( https://nebiocalculator.neb.com/#!/ligation ) is a useful tool to perform these calculations.
Incubate the reactions for at least 00:15:00 at Room temperature .

The reactions are now ready to be transformed directly into competent E. coli cells, using established protocols e.g. E. coli Heat Shock Transformation by Alex Rajewski In our experience, it is helpful to transform all 10 µL of the reactions, although this might depend on the ligation and transformation efficiencies. Subsequent construct verification should also be performed (e.g. PCR, digestion and/or sequencing), before transforming the expression vectors into the expression host.
